blocks:
Ayahs

حمٓ (۱)
Ha, Meem. (1)
وَٱلْكِتَـٰبِ ٱلْمُبِينِ (۲)
By the Manifest Book: (2)
إِنَّا جَعَلْنَـٰهُ قُرْءَٰنًا عَرَبِيًّا لَّعَلَّكُمْ تَعْقِلُونَ (۳)
We have made it an Arabic Quran so that you may exercise your reason, (3)
وَإِنَّهُۥ فِىٓ أُمِّ ٱلْكِتَـٰبِ لَدَيْنَا لَعَلِىٌّ حَكِيمٌ (٤)
and it is sublime and wise with Us in the Mother Book. (4)

Key Points

The characteristics of the Quran as a Divine Book are that: * It is clear and clarifying. «مبین» * It originates from the source of Divine knowledge. «فى امّ الكتاب» * It holds a spiritual status. «لدینا» * Its wording and content are highly ranked and lofty. «علّى» * Its content is full of wisdom and firm. «حكیم» * The word «قرآن» (Quran) derives from the root «قرأ» (qara’a), meaning a readable book. * The word «عربىّ» (Arabic) derives from «عرب» (‘arab), meaning clear and manifest. * The Arabic language means the eloquent and unambiguous language.

Messages

  1. There is a connection between the disjointed letters (Huroof Muqatta'ah) and the revelation of the Quran. This Book is composed of these very alphabet letters. «حم والكتاب»
  2. The Quran is sacred and worthy of being sworn by. «الكتاب»
  3. Swearing by other than Allah is also permissible. «والكتاب»
  4. Although some verses of the Quran are allegorical (Mutashabih), most of its verses are transparent, clear, and understandable. «الكتاب المبین»
  5. The Quran emphasizes that its language is Arabic. «قرآناً عربیّاً»
  6. Choosing the path of God must be through intellect, not mere devotion (ta'abbud). «قرآنا عربیّاً... تعقلون»
  7. Our duty is to propagate the religion, even if there is doubt regarding people's acceptance. «قرآنا عربیّاً لعلّكم تعقلون»
  8. All Divine Books trace back to one single Book, which is the Mother of all Books. «انه فى امّ الكتاب»
  9. The reality of the Quran is in the Preserved Tablet (Lawh Mahfuz) and with Allah. «فى امّ الكتاب لدینا»
  10. Whoever possesses intellect will certainly benefit from the Quran. «لعلكم تعقلون»
  11. Human hands will not reach all the lofty knowledge of the Quran. «لَعلىٌّ»
  12. The passage of time and the advancement of human sciences do not diminish the firmness of the Quran. «حكیم»

Explanations

Portrait of Surah Az-Zukhruf

This Surah has eighty-nine verses, and except for verse 45, the rest of its verses were revealed in Mecca. The name of this Surah is taken from verse 35, where the word «زُخرُف» (Zukhruf) is used. Zukhruf means gold, silver, and adornment. The main topics of this Surah concern the Quran and Prophethood, the reaction of the opponents of the Prophets, proofs of Monotheism (Tawhid) and the struggle against polytheism (Shirk), and a description of a part of the history of the Prophets and the scenes of the Resurrection. An interesting point is that seven consecutive Surahs of the Quran begin with the disjointed letters «حم» (Ha Mim). These are Surahs Ghafir, Fussilat, Ash-Shura, Az-Zukhruf, Ad-Dukhan, Al-Jathiyah, and Al-Ahqaf, which are called the Seven Ha Mim (Hawamim Sab'ah) or the Surahs of Aal Ha Mim.

Ayahs

أَفَنَضْرِبُ عَنكُمُ ٱلذِّكْرَ صَفْحًا أَن كُنتُمْ قَوْمًا مُّسْرِفِينَ (٥)
Shall We keep back the Reminder from you and disregard you because you are an unrestrained lot? (5)
وَكَمْ أَرْسَلْنَا مِن نَّبِىٍّ فِى ٱلْأَوَّلِينَ (٦)
How many a prophet We have sent to the former peoples! (6)
وَمَا يَأْتِيهِم مِّن نَّبِىٍّ إِلَّا كَانُوا۟ بِهِۦ يَسْتَهْزِءُونَ (۷)
There did not come to them any prophet but that they used to deride him. (7)
فَأَهْلَكْنَآ أَشَدَّ مِنْهُم بَطْشًا وَمَضَىٰ مَثَلُ ٱلْأَوَّلِينَ (۸)
So We destroyed those who were stronger than these, and the example of the former peoples has already come to pass. (8)

Key Points

'Safh' (صفح) means turning away, and 'batsh' (بطش) means seizing with power and severity. In the culture of the Quran, israf (extravagance/waste) refers to any action that causes the wasting of divine blessings. Therefore, in these verses, individuals who disregard the heavenly Book are called musrifin (extravagant/transgressors).

Messages

1- The existence of a few obstructors should not prevent the presentation of good. «أفنضرب عنكم الذكر...»

2- The Quran is a means of awakening. «الذكر»

3- Disregard for divine reminders is an example of israf (extravagance/waste). «كنتم قوماً مسرفین»

4- Sending the prophets is one of Allah's divine traditions (Sunan). «كم ارسلنا... فى الاولین»

5- All prophets faced the mockery of opponents, and every prophet must be aware of the situation of previous prophets so that they do not abandon their mission because of the ridicule of a group. «ما یأتیهم من نبىّ الا كانوا به یستهزؤن»

6- The prophets went to the people. «یأتیهم»

7- Familiarity with the difficulties faced by others brings comfort to the heart and a type of spiritual readiness. «ما یأتیهم... الا... یستهزؤن»

8- Mockery has been the constant method of the opponents of the prophets. «كانوا به یستهزؤن»

9- First, the completion of the proof (Hujjah), then the punishment of the obstinate. «یأتیهم من نبى... اهلكنا...»

10- Mocking the men of God leads to destruction and annihilation. «یستهزؤن فاهلكنا»

11- Support the worthy individuals whom you dispatch for a mission. «یستهزؤن - اهلكنا»

12- Break the awe (or might) of the superpowers. «اهلكنا اشد منهم بطشا»

13- The power of the people does not prevent divine wrath. «اهلكنا اشد منهم بطشاً»

14- History and its events are governed by law and possess their own specific traditions (Sunan) and principles. «مضى مثل الاولین»

Ayahs

وَلَئِن سَأَلْتَهُم مَّنْ خَلَقَ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضَ لَيَقُولُنَّ خَلَقَهُنَّ ٱلْعَزِيزُ ٱلْعَلِيمُ (۹)
If you ask them, ‘Who created the heavens and the earth?’ they will surely say, ‘The All-mighty, the All-knowing created them.’ (9)
ٱلَّذِى جَعَلَ لَكُمُ ٱلْأَرْضَ مَهْدًا وَجَعَلَ لَكُمْ فِيهَا سُبُلًا لَّعَلَّكُمْ تَهْتَدُونَ (۱٠)
He, who made the earth a cradle for you, and made for you in it ways so that you may be guided [to your destinations], (10)

Key Points

The polytheists knew the Creator was Allah, but in worship, they worshipped idols. If the segments of the earth had been separated by high mountains and deep valleys, human life would have been paralyzed. Therefore, the possibility of establishing communication routes on earth is one of the divine blessings.

Messages

1- The contempt and mockery of the disbelievers do not prevent inviting them to the truth. «یستهزؤن - لئن سئلتهم»

2- Although arrogance, ignorance, and imitation of ancestors cast a shadow over human thought, the innate nature (Fitrah) fully comprehends the knowledge and power of Allah. «لیقولن... العزیز الحكیم»

3- To flourish the innate nature of knowing Allah, we should recall the blessings. «لیقولن... الّذى جعل لكم»

4- The earth was not habitable by itself; Allah controlled and prepared it for mankind. «جعل لكم الارض مهداً»

5- The purpose of creation is for human benefit. «جعل لكم... جعل لكم»

6- The existence of paths is one of the great divine blessings. «جعل لكم سبلاً»

7- If traveling is based on heedlessness, not taking lessons (Ibrah), there will be no spiritual growth in it. «جعل لكم فیها سبلاً لعلّكم تهتدون»

8- Journeying, traveling, and observing parts of the earth are means of guidance. «سبلاً لعلّكم تهتدون»

Ayahs

وَٱلَّذِى نَزَّلَ مِنَ ٱلسَّمَآءِ مَآءًۢ بِقَدَرٍ فَأَنشَرْنَا بِهِۦ بَلْدَةً مَّيْتًا ۚ كَذَٰلِكَ تُخْرَجُونَ (۱۱)
and who sent down water from the sky in a measured manner, and We revived with it a dead country. Likewise, you shall be raised [from the dead]. (11)
وَٱلَّذِى خَلَقَ ٱلْأَزْوَٰجَ كُلَّهَا وَجَعَلَ لَكُم مِّنَ ٱلْفُلْكِ وَٱلْأَنْعَـٰمِ مَا تَرْكَبُونَ (۱۲)
He created all the kinds and made for you the ships and the cattle such which you ride, (12)

Key Points

In the previous Ayah, the discussion was about Tawhid (Monotheism); in this Ayah, there is a reference to the Resurrection (Ma'ad). The word qadr (قدر) either means measure (size) or it means predestination and planning (taqdir wa barnāmah). Meaning, He sent down the rain in a specific measure, or He sent down the rain according to a plan. (1) Apparently, the intent of al-azwāj (الازواج) is plants. This is because this word is mentioned after the descent of rain and the revival of the earth, and based on other verses of the Quran, the law of pairing (zawjiyyah) is established in them. «أَزْوَاجًا مِّن نَّبَاتٍ شَتَّىٰ» (2) In the previous Ayahs, the discussion was about the path (road); and in these Ayahs, the discussion is about the means of travel and journeying on land and sea. Such as quadrupeds like horses, mules, and camels, or small and large ships.

1) Tafsir Rahnama. 2) Taha, 53.

Messages

1- Every drop of rain is calculated and accounted for. «نَزَّلَ مِنَ السَّمَاءِ مَاءً بِقَدَرٍ»

2- The measured descent of rain is a very great blessing. «بِقَدَرٍ»

3- Allah accomplishes tasks through the means He Himself has created. (He has made rain the cause of the earth's life.) «فَأَنشَرْنَا بِهِ بَلْدَةً مَّيْتًا»

4- Water is the source of life. «أَنشَرْنَا بِهِ بَلْدَةً مَّيْتًا»

5- The season of spring and the growth of plants are examples of the Resurrection. «كَذَٰلِكَ تُخْرَجُونَ»

6- The revival of humans on the Day of Judgment is compulsory. «تُخْرَجُونَ»

7- Pairing (zawjiyyah) is not exclusive to humans or animals. «خَلَقَ الْأَزْوَاجَ كُلَّهَا»

8- Human industry is based on Divine inspiration and the utilization of the laws that He has deposited in creation. «جَعَلَ لَكُم مِّنَ الْفُلْكِ»

9- Whether natural conveyances like animals or industrial conveyances like ships, both are Divine blessings. «جَعَلَ لَكُم... مَّا تَرْكَبُونَ»

Ayahs

لِتَسْتَوُۥا۟ عَلَىٰ ظُهُورِهِۦ ثُمَّ تَذْكُرُوا۟ نِعْمَةَ رَبِّكُمْ إِذَا ٱسْتَوَيْتُمْ عَلَيْهِ وَتَقُولُوا۟ سُبْحَـٰنَ ٱلَّذِى سَخَّرَ لَنَا هَـٰذَا وَمَا كُنَّا لَهُۥ مُقْرِنِينَ (۱۳)
that you may sit on their backs, then remember the blessing of your Lord when you are settled on them, and say, ‘Immaculate is He who has disposed this for us, and we [by ourselves] were no match for it. (13)
وَإِنَّآ إِلَىٰ رَبِّنَا لَمُنقَلِبُونَ (۱٤)
Indeed we shall return to our Lord.’ (14)

Messages

1- The utilization of blessings must be accompanied by the remembrance of Allah and gratitude to Him (not a cause for pride and heedlessness). «لتستوا... ثمّ تذكّروا»

2- Blessings and their suitability to human needs are a manifestation of Divine Lordship. «نعمة ربّكم»

3- Let us learn the way of gratitude from Allah. «تذكّروا نعمة ربّكم... و تقولوا...»

4- Divine glorification (Tasbih) is an example of praise and remembrance of Allah. «تذكّروا نعمة ربّكم... سبحان الّذى»

5- The subservience of the earth and the animal mount for man is among the great Divine blessings. «سخر لنا هذا»

6- If Allah had not made the animals subservient, man would have been incapable of utilizing them. «ما كنّا له مقرنین»

7- Confession of inability is itself an example of gratitude. «و ماكنا له مقرنین»

8- When mounting a vehicle for travel, let us thank Allah and remember the final journey. «تذكّروا نعمة ربّكم اذا استویتم علیه و تقولوا... انا الى ربّنا منقلبون»

Ayahs

وَجَعَلُوا۟ لَهُۥ مِنْ عِبَادِهِۦ جُزْءًا ۚ إِنَّ ٱلْإِنسَـٰنَ لَكَفُورٌ مُّبِينٌ (۱٥)
They ascribe to Him offspring from among His servants! Man is indeed a manifest ingrate. (15)
أَمِ ٱتَّخَذَ مِمَّا يَخْلُقُ بَنَاتٍ وَأَصْفَىٰكُم بِٱلْبَنِينَ (۱٦)
Did He adopt daughters from what He creates while He preferred you with sons? (16)
وَإِذَا بُشِّرَ أَحَدُهُم بِمَا ضَرَبَ لِلرَّحْمَـٰنِ مَثَلًا ظَلَّ وَجْهُهُۥ مُسْوَدًّا وَهُوَ كَظِيمٌ (۱۷)
When one of them is brought the news of what he ascribes to the All-beneficent, his face becomes darkened and he chokes with suppressed rage, [and says] (17)

Key Points

The intent of 'ibādah (His servants) is the angels, whom the polytheists considered to be female. The intent of juz'an (a part) is that the polytheists imagined the angels to be a part of God, like a child.

Messages

1- Reporting superstitious beliefs is permissible if accompanied by a response. «جعلوا له...»

2- Considering the creation a partner in the works of the Creator is manifest ingratitude (or disbelief). «جعلوا له... لكفور مبین»

3- In discussions, one can debate with people based on their own beliefs. «ام اتّخذ...»

4- Considering angels to be daughters, which is commonly seen in paintings and images, is a type of polytheistic superstition. «ام اتّخذ مما یخلق بنات»

5- How ugly it is that a person considers a daughter a disgrace for himself, yet counts the children of God as daughters. «ضرب للرحمن مثلاً»

6- Those who become saddened upon hearing of the birth of a daughter and assume that a son is superior to a daughter are afflicted by a polytheistic superstition. «اذا بشّر احدهم... وجهه مسودّاً و هو كظیم»

7- A person's psychological state affects his body. «ظلّ وجهه مسودّاً»

Ayahs

أَوَمَن يُنَشَّؤُا۟ فِى ٱلْحِلْيَةِ وَهُوَ فِى ٱلْخِصَامِ غَيْرُ مُبِينٍ (۱۸)
‘What! One who is brought up amid ornaments and is inconspicuous in contests?’ (18)
وَجَعَلُوا۟ ٱلْمَلَـٰٓئِكَةَ ٱلَّذِينَ هُمْ عِبَـٰدُ ٱلرَّحْمَـٰنِ إِنَـٰثًا ۚ أَشَهِدُوا۟ خَلْقَهُمْ ۚ سَتُكْتَبُ شَهَـٰدَتُهُمْ وَيُسْـَٔلُونَ (۱۹)
They have made the angels—who are servants of the All-beneficent—females. Were they witness to their creation? Their testimony will be written down and they shall be questioned. (19)

Key Points

We read in Ayah 9 that the polytheists considered the Creator of existence to be Allah, the All-Mighty, the All-Knowing. This Ayah states: You who consider the Creator to be All-Mighty and All-Knowing, why do you assign daughters to Him—who are raised amidst adornment and jewelry, and whose emotions and feelings dominate in discussions? While the requirement of being All-Mighty is firmness and decisiveness, and the requirement of knowledge is reasoning and logic, not emotional or sentimental behavior.

Messages

1- The desire for adornment and jewelry is natural for women and girls. «ینشأ فى الحلیة»

2- Woman is stronger than man in terms of affection and feelings, but weaker in terms of confrontation and dispute. «و هو فى الخصام غیر مبین»

3- Angels are creatures of Allah and His servants, not His children. «عباد الرّحمن»

4- Unlike humans, angels do not have male and female genders. «جعلوا الملائكة... اناثاً»

5- We should not bear witness to what we have not seen ourselves. «اشهدوا خلقهم»

6- All testimonies and statements are recorded by Allah. «ستكتب شهادتهم»

7- Man is responsible for his beliefs. «یسئلون»

8- Superstitious beliefs must be strongly refuted. «ام اتّخذ مما یخلق بنات - اشهدوا خلقهم ستكبت شهادتهم و یسئلون»

Ayah

وَقَالُوا۟ لَوْ شَآءَ ٱلرَّحْمَـٰنُ مَا عَبَدْنَـٰهُم ۗ مَّا لَهُم بِذَٰلِكَ مِنْ عِلْمٍ ۖ إِنْ هُمْ إِلَّا يَخْرُصُونَ (۲٠)
They say, ‘Had the All-beneficent wished, we would not have worshipped them.’ They do not have any knowledge of that, and they do nothing but surmise. (20)

Key Points

“Yakhruṣūn” (یخرصون) is derived from “kharṣ” (خَرص), meaning speech based on conjecture and speculation. The Quran, in Surah An-Nahl, Ayah 35, and Surah Al-An'am, Ayah 148, also recounts this meaning from the polytheists who used to say: If Allah had not willed it, we would not have been polytheists.

Messages

1- Wrongdoers seek to justify their actions and use the Will of Allah as an excuse. «و قالوا لو شاء الرّحمن ما عبدناهم»

2- We should not mistake Allah's granting of respite as a sign of His satisfaction. «لو شاء الرّحمن ما عبدناهم»

3- The angels were among those worshipped by the polytheists. «عبدناهم»

4- Speaking without knowledge (especially concerning matters of faith) is condemned. «مالهم بذلك من علم»

5- Any individual or society devoid of knowledge resorts to illusions and conjectures. «ان هم الاّ یخرصون»

Ayahs

أَمْ ءَاتَيْنَـٰهُمْ كِتَـٰبًا مِّن قَبْلِهِۦ فَهُم بِهِۦ مُسْتَمْسِكُونَ (۲۱)
Did We give them a Book before this, so that they are holding fast to it? (21)
بَلْ قَالُوٓا۟ إِنَّا وَجَدْنَآ ءَابَآءَنَا عَلَىٰٓ أُمَّةٍ وَإِنَّا عَلَىٰٓ ءَاثَـٰرِهِم مُّهْتَدُونَ (۲۲)
No, they said, ‘We found our fathers following a creed, and we are indeed guided in their footsteps.’ (22)

Messages

1- Polytheism and superstition have neither a rational basis, «مالهم بذلك من علم», nor a transmitted support from the heavenly Books. «ام آتیناهم كتابا»

2- The root of polytheism and superstition is blind imitation. «انّا وجدنا آبائنا على امّة»

3- Following the customs, traditions, and beliefs of ancestors is condemned if it is not supported by reason or a heavenly Book. «انّا وجدنا آبائنا على امّة»

4- The predecessors and ancestors who established wrong traditions are also responsible for the subsequent generations. «انّا على آثارهم مهتدون»

5- The spread of national customs and traditions sometimes portrays an illogical person as a guided person. «انّا على آثارهم مهتدون»

6- Nationalism promotes blind imitation and ignorant fanaticism. «انّا على آثارهم مهتدون»

Ayah

وَكَذَٰلِكَ مَآ أَرْسَلْنَا مِن قَبْلِكَ فِى قَرْيَةٍ مِّن نَّذِيرٍ إِلَّا قَالَ مُتْرَفُوهَآ إِنَّا وَجَدْنَآ ءَابَآءَنَا عَلَىٰٓ أُمَّةٍ وَإِنَّا عَلَىٰٓ ءَاثَـٰرِهِم مُّقْتَدُونَ (۲۳)
So it has been that We did not send any warner to a town before you, without its affluent ones saying, ‘We found our fathers following a creed and we are indeed following in their footsteps.’ (23)

Key Points

The term mutraf (the affluent/extravagant) is derived from turfah, meaning abundant blessing, and refers to someone whom these blessings have made arrogant and intoxicated.

In the previous Ayah, we read that the common people assume the path and method of their ancestors is correct, and they follow them to attain guidance. «على آثارهم مهتدون» (upon their footsteps they are guided). However, this Ayah states: For the affluent (mutrafīn), luxury and wealth are the concern, not attaining guidance. Therefore, following the customs and traditions of their ancestors is not for guidance. «على آثارهم مقتدون» (upon their footsteps they are following/imitating).

The word «بعدك» (after you) is never used in the Quran for the Prophet of Islam, but rather «قبلك» (before you) is used. This is a sign that the Prophet of Islam is the last Divine Messenger.

Messages

1- Relying on the method of ancestors has been the tradition of the deviated throughout history. «و كذلك»

2- The Prophets had opponents from the affluent class who offered similar and uniform excuses. «ما ارسلنا من قبلك... الاّ قال مترفوها»

3- Paying attention to the difficulties faced by those who came before brings peace to a person. «ما ارسلنا من قبلك...»

4- The most important role of the Prophets is warning. «من نذیر»

5- Intoxication with wealth and luxury both leads to transgression and provides the ground for blind imitation and undue fanaticism. «قال مترفوها انا وجدنا آبائنا... على آثارهم مقتدون»

6- The ideas of predecessors are subject to review and examination, and absolute, unquestioning imitation of them is meaningless. «انّا على آثارهم مقتدون»

Ayahs

۞ قَـٰلَ أَوَلَوْ جِئْتُكُم بِأَهْدَىٰ مِمَّا وَجَدتُّمْ عَلَيْهِ ءَابَآءَكُمْ ۖ قَالُوٓا۟ إِنَّا بِمَآ أُرْسِلْتُم بِهِۦ كَـٰفِرُونَ (۲٤)
He would say, ‘What! Even if I bring you a better guidance than what you found your fathers following?!’ They would say, ‘We indeed disbelieve in what you are sent with.’ (24)
فَٱنتَقَمْنَا مِنْهُمْ ۖ فَٱنظُرْ كَيْفَ كَانَ عَـٰقِبَةُ ٱلْمُكَذِّبِينَ (۲٥)
Thereupon We took vengeance on them; so observe how was the fate of the deniers. (25)

Messages

1- In forbidding evil (Nahy 'anil Munkar), one must first present a better good (Ma'ruf) and then request the abandonment of the evil. «جئتكم بأهدى»

2- One of the ways to introduce a school of thought is by comparing the teachings of different schools. «جئتكم بأهدى» (In their invitation, the Prophets presented reason and intellect, while the opponents presented imitation and tribalism.)

3- Superior guidance is the criterion for selection, not anything else. «جئتكم بأهدى»

4- Although we know that there is no guidance in idol worship for the call to Monotheism (Tawhid) to be superior guidance, good argumentation (Jidal) requires dealing gently with opponents when inviting them to the truth. «بأهدى مما وجدتم»

5- When obstinacy sets in, it removes the power to distinguish what is superior. «اهدى مما وجدتم...كافرون»

6- Dogmatism and fanaticism cause the disregard of truths. «بما ارسلتم به كافرون»

7- The end of disbelief and obstinacy is destruction. «فانتقمنا منهم»

8- Divine retribution occurs after the completion of the proof (Itmam al-Hujjah). «جئتكم بأهدى... انا... كافرون فانتقمنا»

9- Vengeance and severity, when appropriate, do not contradict Divine mercy and compassion. «فانتقمنا منهم»

10- Let us learn lessons from the bitter events of history. «فانظر... عاقبة المكذّبین»

11- The final outcome is the criterion, not fleeting appearances. «عاقبة المكذّبین»

Ayahs

وَإِذْ قَالَ إِبْرَٰهِيمُ لِأَبِيهِ وَقَوْمِهِۦٓ إِنَّنِى بَرَآءٌ مِّمَّا تَعْبُدُونَ (۲٦)
When Abraham said to his father and his people, ‘I repudiate what you worship, (26)
إِلَّا ٱلَّذِى فَطَرَنِى فَإِنَّهُۥ سَيَهْدِينِ (۲۷)
excepting Him who originated me; indeed He will guide me.’ (27)
وَجَعَلَهَا كَلِمَةًۢ بَاقِيَةً فِى عَقِبِهِۦ لَعَلَّهُمْ يَرْجِعُونَ (۲۸)
He made it a lasting word among his posterity so that they may come back [to the right path]. (28)

Key Points

In the previous verses, the logic of the idolaters was following their ancestors. These verses state that Abraham strongly opposed the path of his guardian, who was his uncle. Regarding who the agent (fa'il) of "ja'ala" (made/placed) is in "و جعلها كلمة باقیة" (and He made it a lasting word), there are two possibilities: Allah or Abraham. It can be said that Allah kept Monotheism alive in his progeny in return for Abraham's faith, and it can also be said that Abraham left the cry of Monotheism and dissociation from polytheism as a legacy for his descendants. Imam Ali عليه السلام said: The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله is from the progeny of Abraham, and we, the Ahl al-Bayt, are also from the progeny of Abraham and Muhammad. (3)

3) Tafsir Burhan.

Messages

1- Let us not forget the memory of great figures, their words, actions, and decisive stances. "و اذ قال" (And [mention] when he said)

2- Ethnic, tribal, or partisan affiliations should not have a negative impact on our belief and thought. "قال لابیه و قومه" (He said to his father and his people)

3- Man has a special responsibility towards his relatives, and their guidance is a priority. "قال لابیه" (He said to his father)

4- Dissociation (Bara'ah) from polytheism is a monotheistic slogan. "اننى براء مما تعبدون" (Indeed, I am disassociated from that which you worship)

5- He who created will also guide. "فطرنى... سیهدین" (He Who created me... He will guide me)

6- All people, even the Prophets, are in need of Divine guidance. "سیهدین" (He will guide me)

7- Initial faith is not enough; Divine guidance must be continuous. "سیهدین" (He will guide me)

8- The cry of Monotheism remains in history, even if the audience of that day did not listen. "كلمة باقیة فى عقبه" (a lasting word among his descendants)

9- Allah is the Preserver and Guardian of the religion of Monotheism throughout history. "جعلها كلمة باقیة" (He made it a lasting word)

10- The progeny of Prophet Abraham are the standard-bearers of Monotheism throughout history. "جعلها كلمة باقیة فى عقبه" (He made it a lasting word among his descendants)

11- The actions of fathers are effective in the destiny of their progeny. "جعلها كلمة باقیة" (He made it a lasting word)

Ayahs

بَلْ مَتَّعْتُ هَـٰٓؤُلَآءِ وَءَابَآءَهُمْ حَتَّىٰ جَآءَهُمُ ٱلْحَقُّ وَرَسُولٌ مُّبِينٌ (۲۹)
Indeed, I provided for these and their fathers until the truth and a manifest apostle came to them. (29)
وَلَمَّا جَآءَهُمُ ٱلْحَقُّ قَالُوا۟ هَـٰذَا سِحْرٌ وَإِنَّا بِهِۦ كَـٰفِرُونَ (۳٠)
But when the truth came to them, they said, ‘This is magic, and we indeed disbelieve in it.’ (30)
وَقَالُوا۟ لَوْلَا نُزِّلَ هَـٰذَا ٱلْقُرْءَانُ عَلَىٰ رَجُلٍ مِّنَ ٱلْقَرْيَتَيْنِ عَظِيمٍ (۳۱)
And they said, ‘Why was not this Quran sent down to some great man from the two cities?’ (31)

Key Points

Some disbelievers had an undue expectation and said: Since so-and-so is wealthy, the revelation should have been sent down to him, whereas material superiority is not proof of spiritual superiority. Mecca and Ta'if were the two important cities of the Arabs of Hijaz during the era of the Prophetic mission (Bi'that).

Messages

1- The tradition (Sunnah) of Allah is to grant success (or enjoyment) to both the believer and the disbeliever and to grant respite to all people. «متعت هؤلاء و اباءهم»

2- For the completion of the argument (Itmām al-Hujjah), both a Book and a leader are necessary. «جاءهم الحقّ و رسول مبین»

3- The Quran contains speech that is entirely truth and reality. «جاءهم الحق»

4- The propagation of religion and the message must be clear and eloquent. «رسول مبین»

5- The effect of the Quran on the hearts and souls of the disbelievers was such that they called it magic and sorcery. «هذا سحر»

6- Man is inherently a justifier (one who rationalizes). The disbelievers leveled the accusation of magic to justify their disbelief. «هذا سحر و انّا به كافرون»

7- Those who make excuses speak differently at every moment; one time they say: the Quran is magic, and another time they say: why was the Quran not sent down to so-and-so? «هذا سحر... لولا نزل»

8- Greatness, according to some people, lies in possessing wealth and status. «رجلٍ... عظیم»

Ayah

أَهُمْ يَقْسِمُونَ رَحْمَتَ رَبِّكَ ۚ نَحْنُ قَسَمْنَا بَيْنَهُم مَّعِيشَتَهُمْ فِى ٱلْحَيَوٰةِ ٱلدُّنْيَا ۚ وَرَفَعْنَا بَعْضَهُمْ فَوْقَ بَعْضٍ دَرَجَـٰتٍ لِّيَتَّخِذَ بَعْضُهُم بَعْضًا سُخْرِيًّا ۗ وَرَحْمَتُ رَبِّكَ خَيْرٌ مِّمَّا يَجْمَعُونَ (۳۲)
Is it they who dispense the mercy of your Lord? It is We who have dispensed among them their livelihood in the present life, and raised some of them above others in rank, so that some may take others into service, and your Lord’s mercy is better than what they amass. (32)

Key Points

The superiority of some humans over others is of two types: - One is superiority in physical and intellectual structure and talents. This causes a feeling of mutual need and service to one another, and thus society is formed. Man has no role in this superiority. «رفعنا بعضهم فوق بعض درجات» - The other type is the superiority that Allah grants based on human effort, similar to the Ayah: «یرفع اللّه الّذین آمنوا منكم والّذین اوتوا العلم درجات»(4) (Allah will raise those who have believed among you and those who were given knowledge, by degrees), where Allah grants superiority to those who pursued knowledge and faith.

When the livelihood of humans is in the hands of Allah, how can some expect the station of prophethood to be theirs, and that they should decide upon whom the revelation descends?

The differences that exist among people should lead to connection, cooperation, and the employment of some by others, not to exploitation, boasting, or the humiliation of others.

«سُخریاً» means that one group of humans employs another group and hires them.

4) Al-Mujadilah, 11.

Messages

1- Man must know his worth and place and not overstep his bounds. (The selection of the Prophet is within the domain of Divine Will, not the expectation or demand of the people.) «اهم یقسمون رحمة ربّك»

2- Prophethood is a special Divine mercy. «رحمة ربّك»

3- No one has a claim upon Allah; everything that exists is His grace and mercy. «رحمة ربّك»

4- Sustenance (Rizq) is only in the hand of Allah. «نحن قسمنا بینهم معیشتهم»

5- Physical and intellectual superiorities should not cause your arrogance, because all of them are from Allah. «نحن قسمنا - رفعنا»

6- The idea of a classless society is nothing more than an illusion. (The Wise God Himself has established differences so that people feel a need for one another and grow through mutual assistance within the framework of society.) «رفعنا بعضهم فوق بعض درجات»

7- Being hired, hiring others, and the employment of some humans by others is permissible and legitimate. «لیتخذ بعضهم بعضاً سخریّاً»

8- A stable society and a healthy economy depend on cooperation and utilizing the capabilities of various human resources. «لیتخذ بعضهم بعضاً سخریّاً»

9- Mercy is among the affairs of Lordship (Rububiyyah). «رحمة ربّك»

10- We should not compare revelation and prophethood with material things. «رحمة ربّك خیر مما یجمعون»

11- Material superiorities should not distract you from Divine mercy. «و رحمة ربّك خیر مما یجمعون»

Ayah

وَلَوْلَآ أَن يَكُونَ ٱلنَّاسُ أُمَّةً وَٰحِدَةً لَّجَعَلْنَا لِمَن يَكْفُرُ بِٱلرَّحْمَـٰنِ لِبُيُوتِهِمْ سُقُفًا مِّن فِضَّةٍ وَمَعَارِجَ عَلَيْهَا يَظْهَرُونَ (۳۳)
Were it not [for the danger] that mankind would be one community, We would have made for those who defy the All-beneficent, silver roofs for their houses and [silver] stairways by which they ascend, (33)

Key Points

The glitter and glamour of the world are so worthless that Allah says in this Ayah: We are ready to give the world in abundance to the disbelievers, but because people's intellect is in their eyes (they judge by appearances), if We give it to the disbelievers, everyone will incline towards disbelief. Not only is the equality of all human beings in economic and livelihood matters not willed by Allah, but even in matters of belief, things should not move in a direction where the right to choose is taken away from individuals, leading us to assume that if all people in the world adopt one thought and one belief, we have reached the desired goal.

Messages

1- In granting and withdrawing blessings, the capacity of the society must be observed. «لولا... لجعلنا...»

2- A unified nation (Ummah Wahidah) is valuable only if it is based on faith (Iman), not disbelief (Kufr). «لولا ان یكون الناس امّة واحدة»

3- Wealth is not a sign of honor (Izzah) in the sight of Allah. «لجعلنا لمن یكفر بالرّحمن لبیوتهم سقفاً من فضّة»

Ayahs

وَلِبُيُوتِهِمْ أَبْوَٰبًا وَسُرُرًا عَلَيْهَا يَتَّكِـُٔونَ (۳٤)
and [silver] doors for their houses and [silver] couches on which they recline, (34)
وَزُخْرُفًا ۚ وَإِن كُلُّ ذَٰلِكَ لَمَّا مَتَـٰعُ ٱلْحَيَوٰةِ ٱلدُّنْيَا ۚ وَٱلْـَٔاخِرَةُ عِندَ رَبِّكَ لِلْمُتَّقِينَ (۳٥)
and ornaments of gold; yet all that would be nothing but the wares of the life of this world, and the Hereafter is for the Godwary near your Lord. (35)

Messages

1- In the view of the disbelievers, value lies in adornment and jewelry, while in the view of the pious, value lies in eternal happiness. «و لبیوتهم ابواباً... والآخرة عند ربّك للمتّقین»

2- Instead of the pious focusing on the adornment and jewelry of their houses in this world, they focus on the Hereafter, which Allah Himself has prepared all these things (rewards) for. «من یكفر بالرّحمن لبیوتهم سقفاً من فضّة... و زخرفاً... و الآخرة... للمتّقین»

3- Proximity to the Lord and the grace of His presence cannot be compared to anything. «عند ربّك»

Ayahs

وَمَن يَعْشُ عَن ذِكْرِ ٱلرَّحْمَـٰنِ نُقَيِّضْ لَهُۥ شَيْطَـٰنًا فَهُوَ لَهُۥ قَرِينٌ (۳٦)
We assign a devil to be the companion of him who turns a blind eye to the remembrance of the All-beneficent. (36)
وَإِنَّهُمْ لَيَصُدُّونَهُمْ عَنِ ٱلسَّبِيلِ وَيَحْسَبُونَ أَنَّهُم مُّهْتَدُونَ (۳۷)
Indeed they bar them from the way [of Allah], while they suppose that they are [rightly] guided. (37)

Key Points

The previous verses spoke about the disbelievers (kuffār) and their immersion in materialism. This verse explains one of the consequences of being submerged in gold and adornments.

Ya'sha (یعش) derives from 'ashw (عشو), meaning an affliction in the eye that causes weak sight. When this word is accompanied by the particle 'an (عن), it means to turn away or shun.

The policy of Satan is step-by-step: khuṭuwāt (خطوات) (5). The first stage is the casting of temptation (whisper). Waswasa ilayh (6). In the second stage, he makes contact. Massahum ṭā’if (7). The third stage, he penetrates the heart. Fī ṣudūr al-nās (8). The fourth stage, he remains in the soul. Fa huwa lahu qarīn (9). The fifth stage, he makes the person a member of his party. Ḥizb ash-Shayṭān (10). The sixth stage, he becomes the guardian (master) of the person. Wa man yattakhidh ash-Shayṭāna waliyyan (11). The seventh stage, the person himself becomes a devil. Shayāṭīn al-ins wa... (12).

Imam Ali عليه السلام says in Nahj al-Balaghah: Satan lays eggs in the human soul. Fa bāḍa wa farrakha fī ṣudūrihim (13).

Consequences of turning away from the remembrance of Allah: 1. Being held back from the path of good. Layaṣuddūnahum ‘an as-sabīl. 2. Intellectual deviation, where he imagines that he has been guided. Yaḥsabūna annahum muhtadūn. 3. He is no longer receptive to admonition. Wa idhā dhukkirū lā yadhkurūn (14). 4. He does not repent because he does not consider himself wrong. 5. He has a miserable life. Wa man a‘raḍa ‘an dhikrī fa inna lahu ma‘īshatan ḍankā (15). 6. Whoever is blind to seeing the truth in this world (ya‘sha), will also be resurrected blind in the Hereafter. Man kāna fī hādhihi a‘mā fa huwa fī al-ākhirati a‘mā (16).

The accompanying devil (Satan) might be bad friends, a spouse, a child, or a corrupt partner. For this reason, the word Shayṭān (Satan) is mentioned in the indefinite form (nakirah) so that it includes every type of devil.

Prayer (Salat) is the remembrance of the All-Merciful (Rahman): Aqim aṣ-ṣalāta li-dhikrī (17). The Quran is the remembrance of the All-Merciful: Naḥnu nazzalnā adh-dhikr (18). Therefore, whoever turns away from prayer and the Quran falls under the domination of the accompanying devil. Fa huwa lahu qarīn.

(5) Al-Baqarah, 168. (6) Taha, 120. (7) Al-A'raf, 201. (8) An-Nas, 5. (9) Az-Zukhruf, 36. (10) Al-Mujadilah, 19. (11) An-Nisa, 119. (12) Al-An'am, 112. (13) Nahj al-Balaghah, Sermon 7. (14) As-Saffat, 13. (15) Taha, 124. (16) Al-Isra, 72. (17) Taha, 14. (18) Al-Hijr, 9.

Messages

1- The domination of Satan is due to our own actions. Wa man ya‘shu... nuqayyiḍ.

2- The heart is either the place for the All-Merciful (Rahman) or for Satan. Wa man ya‘shu ‘an dhikr ar-Raḥmān nuqayyiḍ lahu shayṭānan.

3- The punishment for turning away from the source of mercy is servitude to the dominating devil. Fa huwa lahu qarīn.

4- Satan's effort to hold back humans from the path of truth is serious. Innahum layaṣuddūnahum.

5- Worse than practical deviation is intellectual deviation. Yaḥsabūna innahum muhtadūn.

Ayahs

حَتَّىٰٓ إِذَا جَآءَنَا قَالَ يَـٰلَيْتَ بَيْنِى وَبَيْنَكَ بُعْدَ ٱلْمَشْرِقَيْنِ فَبِئْسَ ٱلْقَرِينُ (۳۸)
When he comes to Us, he will say, ‘I wish there had been between me and you the distance between the east and the west! What an evil companion [you are]!’ (38)
وَلَن يَنفَعَكُمُ ٱلْيَوْمَ إِذ ظَّلَمْتُمْ أَنَّكُمْ فِى ٱلْعَذَابِ مُشْتَرِكُونَ (۳۹)
‘Today that will be of no avail to you. As you did wrong, so will you share in the punishment.’ (39)

Key Points

The criminals on the Day of Judgment will wish that they could at least be far from the devils in Hell, but the Quran states: they are partners with the devils even in the torment and will not be separated from them.

Messages

1- Regrets in this world can be fruitful, but in the Hereafter, they are useless except for sorrow. «یا لیت...»

2- On the Day of Judgment, when the veils are lifted, many beloved things become detested and assumptions prove false. «یحسبون انهم مهتدون حتّى اذا جاءنا»

3- On the Day of Judgment, the devils will be gathered with mankind. «یا لیت بینى و بینك»

4- Companions in this world will be companions in the Hereafter. «فهو له قرین... فبئس القرین»

5- Being a companion (قرین) to Satan on the Day of Judgment is itself a type of torment. «فبئس القرین»

6- Turning away from the remembrance of Allah, which is the ground for Satan's dominance, is oppression against oneself and the prophets. «اذ ظلمتم»

7- Contrary to worldly prisons, where the hardship of a general prison is less than solitary confinement, the generality of the torment on the Day of Judgment is of no benefit. «لن ینفعكم... انّكم فى العذاب مشتركون»

Ayah

أَفَأَنتَ تُسْمِعُ ٱلصُّمَّ أَوْ تَهْدِى ٱلْعُمْىَ وَمَن كَانَ فِى ضَلَـٰلٍ مُّبِينٍ (٤٠)
Can you, then, make the deaf hear or guide the blind and those who are in manifest error? (40)

Key Points

The word of truth is effective only among the God-fearing, living hearts, not others. We read in the Qur'an: «لِيُنذِرَ مَن كَانَ حَيًّا» (19), so that He may warn every living heart. «إِنَّمَا تُنذِرُ مَنِ اتَّبَعَ الذِّكْرَ وَخَشِيَ الرَّحْمَٰنَ» (20) He only warns those who follow the Reminder (Dhikr) and fear the All-Merciful (Al-Rahman).

19) Yasin, 70. 20) Yasin, 11.

Messages

1- If the ground for acceptance is not present, even the word of the Messenger of Allah will have no effect. «أَفَأَنتَ»

2- The Prophet had a burning desire (zeal) for the guidance of the people. «أَفَأَنتَ تُسْمِعُ... أَوْ تَهْدِي»

3- Association with Satan causes inner deafness and blindness. «الصُّمُّ - الْعُمْيُ»

Ayahs

فَإِمَّا نَذْهَبَنَّ بِكَ فَإِنَّا مِنْهُم مُّنتَقِمُونَ (٤۱)
We will indeed take vengeance on them, whether We take you away (41)
أَوْ نُرِيَنَّكَ ٱلَّذِى وَعَدْنَـٰهُمْ فَإِنَّا عَلَيْهِم مُّقْتَدِرُونَ (٤۲)
or show you what We have promised them, for indeed We hold them in Our power. (42)

Key Points

The meaning of Divine retribution (intiqām) is His just punishment, not malice or resentment, which are usually the roots of human vengeance.

Messages

1- The death and life of every human being, including the Prophets, are in the hands of Allah. «فامّا نذهبنّ بك»

2- The punishment of the disbelievers is a Divine tradition (Sunnah). «انّا منهم منتقمون»

3- Let the disbelievers not assume that they will not be punished while the Prophet is alive, or that there will be no punishment once the Prophet passes away. «فامّا نذهبن بك... او نرینّك»

4- The time of the descent of Allah's wrath is in His hands and is based on wisdom, whether it occurs during the time of the Prophet or after his passing. «نذهبّن... او نرینّك... فانا علیهم مقتدرون»

Ayahs

فَٱسْتَمْسِكْ بِٱلَّذِىٓ أُوحِىَ إِلَيْكَ ۖ إِنَّكَ عَلَىٰ صِرَٰطٍ مُّسْتَقِيمٍ (٤۳)
So hold fast to what has been revealed to you. Indeed, you are on a straight path. (43)
وَإِنَّهُۥ لَذِكْرٌ لَّكَ وَلِقَوْمِكَ ۖ وَسَوْفَ تُسْـَٔلُونَ (٤٤)
Indeed it is a reminder for you and your people, and soon you will be questioned. (44)

Key Points

Two meanings have been mentioned for the word «ذكر» (Dhikr): one is remembrance (tadhakkur) and recollection, and the other is fame, greatness, and good repute. That is, this Qur'an is either a cause for remembrance and the removal of heedlessness, or it is a cause for your fame throughout history, granting greatness to you and your Ummah.

Messages

1- The blindness and hardness of people's hearts should not affect the Prophet. «فاستمسك بالّذى»

2- Whoever is on the straight path and connected to revelation must be serious and energetic in his work. «فاستمسك بالّذى اوحى الیك»

3- We should not doubt the truthfulness of our path due to people's lack of acceptance. «انك على صراط مستقیم»

4- The Prophet is infallible (ma'sum). «انك على صراط مستقیم»

Ayah

وَسْـَٔلْ مَنْ أَرْسَلْنَا مِن قَبْلِكَ مِن رُّسُلِنَآ أَجَعَلْنَا مِن دُونِ ٱلرَّحْمَـٰنِ ءَالِهَةً يُعْبَدُونَ (٤٥)
Ask those of Our apostles We have sent before you: Did We set up any gods to be worshipped besides the All-beneficent? (45)

Key Points

The word "al-Rahman" has been used repeatedly in the previous verses. Perhaps this is an indication that abandoning the source of Mercy and going after other than Him is not a condition of fairness.

Messages

1- Everyone is in need of reminder/admonition. «ذكرٌ لك و لقومك»

2- First, admonition for oneself, then for the people. «ذكرٌ لك و لقومك»

3- Everyone is responsible regarding the Quran. «و سوف تسئلون»

4- Monotheism (Tawhid) is the common principle of all religions, and the Prophet of Islam is the continuation of the call of the previous prophets. «و اسئل من ارسلنا من قبلك»

5- The Prophet of Islam has oversight over all previous prophets. «و اسئل من ارسلنا من قبلك من رسلنا»

Ayahs

وَلَقَدْ أَرْسَلْنَا مُوسَىٰ بِـَٔايَـٰتِنَآ إِلَىٰ فِرْعَوْنَ وَمَلَإِي۟هِۦ فَقَالَ إِنِّى رَسُولُ رَبِّ ٱلْعَـٰلَمِينَ (٤٦)
Certainly We sent Moses with Our signs to Pharaoh and his elite. He said, ‘I am indeed an apostle of the Lord of all the worlds.’ (46)
فَلَمَّا جَآءَهُم بِـَٔايَـٰتِنَآ إِذَا هُم مِّنْهَا يَضْحَكُونَ (٤۷)
But when he brought them Our signs, they indeed laughed at them. (47)

Key Points

In the Quran, the story of Moses, Pharaoh, and the Children of Israel is repeated because the destiny of Moses and his people resembles the destiny of the Prophet of Islam and the people of Mecca. Pharaoh made the excuse that Moses was poor while he possessed the throne and crown. Similarly, the leaders of the disbelievers of Mecca considered the Prophet of Islam poor and an orphan, while they regarded themselves as possessing wealth and status.

Messages

1- Prophets, in addition to personal perfections, must possess miracles. «موسى بایاتنا»

2- In tribal conditions, one must approach the tribes/people. «قال لقومه» But in governmental conditions, one must approach the government. «الى فرعون» (In the method of propagation, one must first address the obstacles and reform the elements whose change causes societal change.)

3- Tyrants do not possess power alone; it is their entourage that gives them strength. «فرعون و ملائه»

4- Laughter and mockery are signs of the hollowness, frivolity, and rabble-rousing of the opponents. «اذا هم یضحكون»

Ayahs

وَمَا نُرِيهِم مِّنْ ءَايَةٍ إِلَّا هِىَ أَكْبَرُ مِنْ أُخْتِهَا ۖ وَأَخَذْنَـٰهُم بِٱلْعَذَابِ لَعَلَّهُمْ يَرْجِعُونَ (٤۸)
We did not show them a sign but it was greater than the other, and We visited on them punishment so that they might come back. (48)
وَقَالُوا۟ يَـٰٓأَيُّهَ ٱلسَّاحِرُ ٱدْعُ لَنَا رَبَّكَ بِمَا عَهِدَ عِندَكَ إِنَّنَا لَمُهْتَدُونَ (٤۹)
They would say, ‘O magician! Invoke your Lord for us by the covenant He has made with you [to remove this scourge]. We will indeed be guided [when it is removed].’ (49)
فَلَمَّا كَشَفْنَا عَنْهُمُ ٱلْعَذَابَ إِذَا هُمْ يَنكُثُونَ (٥٠)
But when We lifted the punishment from them, behold, they would break their pledge. (50)

Key Points

In Surah Al-A'raf, Ayah 135, He also says: As soon as We removed Our wrath from them, they broke the covenant of submission they had made. «فلمّا كشفنا عنهم الرجز الى اجل هم بالغوه اذا هم ینكثون»

Messages

1- For stubborn spirits, continuous argumentation and miracles are necessary. «و ما نریهم من آیة الاّ هى اكبر من اختها»

2- Continuous miracles are a sign of Allah's favor toward the guidance of people. «آیة الاّ هى اكبر من اختها»

3- First the completion of the proof (ultimatum), then the punishment. «نریهم من آیة... اخذنا»

4- In the method of propagation (Da'wah), one must first pass through the ordinary stages, then the higher stages. «هى اكبر من اختها»

5- Divine wrath in this world is for the attention and return of the people. «لعلهم یرجعون»

6- Base individuals do not cease humiliating others, even when they are in distress and under pressure. «یا ایّها الساحر»

7- Criminals, deep within their souls, know the friends of Allah (Awliya') to be those whose prayers are answered. «ادع لنا ربّك»

8- Prophethood is a Divine covenant. «عهد عندك»

9- Man makes a commitment when in distress «اننا لمهتدون», but after the problem is solved, he breaks the covenant. «ینكثون»

10- When danger is sensed, the innate nature (Fitrah) of knowing God awakens in man. «اننا لمهتدون»

Ayahs

وَنَادَىٰ فِرْعَوْنُ فِى قَوْمِهِۦ قَالَ يَـٰقَوْمِ أَلَيْسَ لِى مُلْكُ مِصْرَ وَهَـٰذِهِ ٱلْأَنْهَـٰرُ تَجْرِى مِن تَحْتِىٓ ۖ أَفَلَا تُبْصِرُونَ (٥۱)
And Pharaoh proclaimed to his people, saying, ‘O my people! Do not the kingdom of Egypt and these rivers that run at my feet belong to me? Do you not perceive? (51)
أَمْ أَنَا۠ خَيْرٌ مِّنْ هَـٰذَا ٱلَّذِى هُوَ مَهِينٌ وَلَا يَكَادُ يُبِينُ (٥۲)
Am I not better than this wretch who cannot even speak clearly? (52)

Key Points

The phrase "أنا خیر" (I am better) appears in the Quran, uttered once by Iblis and once by Pharaoh.

The enemy exaggerates the smallest point of weakness. The speech of Prophet Moses was slightly heavy (or difficult); he himself said, "My brother is more eloquent than me," «واخى هارون هو افصح منّى»(21). But Pharaoh magnifies this issue and says, «لا یكاد یبین» (He can scarcely express himself clearly), meaning Moses cannot speak clearly.

In these verses, fear, boasting, tyranny, arrogance, false belief, reliance on wealth, and the use of pomp and glitter—which are characteristics of the tyrant (طاغوت)—are clearly visible.

Messages

1- Tyrants resort to propaganda when facing danger. «و نادى فرعون» (And Pharaoh proclaimed).

2- Pharaoh exploited the emotions of the people. «یا قوم» (O my people).

3- Since the tyrant lacks logic, he relies on his palace and wealth. «ألیس لى ملك مصر» (Does not the dominion of Egypt belong to me?).

4- Tyrants demand that people's intellect be in their eyes (i.e., they should only judge by appearances). «أفلا تبصرون» (Will you not then see?).

5- Self-centeredness is a prominent characteristic of the tyrant. «ألیس لى - ام انا خیر» (Does not belong to me? - Or am I better?).

6- Belittling others is a characteristic of the tyrant. «هو مهین - لا یكاد یبین» (He is contemptible - He can scarcely express himself clearly).

7- Considering others contemptible due to simple clothing and appearance is Pharaonic behavior. «هو مَهین» (He is contemptible).

Ayahs

فَلَوْلَآ أُلْقِىَ عَلَيْهِ أَسْوِرَةٌ مِّن ذَهَبٍ أَوْ جَآءَ مَعَهُ ٱلْمَلَـٰٓئِكَةُ مُقْتَرِنِينَ (٥۳)
Why no bracelets of gold have been thrown to him, nor any angels accompany him as escorts?’ (53)
فَٱسْتَخَفَّ قَوْمَهُۥ فَأَطَاعُوهُ ۚ إِنَّهُمْ كَانُوا۟ قَوْمًا فَـٰسِقِينَ (٥٤)
Thus did he mislead his people and they obeyed him. Indeed, they were a transgressing lot. (54)
فَلَمَّآ ءَاسَفُونَا ٱنتَقَمْنَا مِنْهُمْ فَأَغْرَقْنَـٰهُمْ أَجْمَعِينَ (٥٥)
So when they roused Our wrath, We took vengeance on them and drowned them all. (55)
فَجَعَلْنَـٰهُمْ سَلَفًا وَمَثَلًا لِّلْـَٔاخِرِينَ (٥٦)
Thus We made them the vanguard and an example for posterity. (56)

Key Points

Aswirah (أسورة) is the plural of siwār (سِوار), meaning bracelet. The rule of governors over the people in a false system is based on belittling (istikhfāf) and humiliating the people. However, the obedience of the people in the true system is either based on choosing the best, "یستمعون القول فیتّبعون احسنه" (22), and that too is accompanied by love, "لو كنتَ فظّاً غلیظ القلب لانفضّوا من حولك" (23) (If you had been rude and hard-hearted, they would certainly have dispersed from around you).

Imam Sadiq عليه السلام was asked about the interpretation of the word āsafoonā (آسفونا). He replied: Allah does not feel regret (or sorrow) like us humans, but He has representatives (Awliyā) whose regret or satisfaction brings about His wrath and satisfaction. (24)

The past is called salaf (سَلَف) and the future is called khalaf (خَلف). That which is a model is called mathal (مَثَل). Individuals who are cited as examples, similar to proverbs, are also called mathal. "فجعلناهم سلفاً و مثلاً للاخرین"

21) القصص, 34. 22) الزمر, 18. 23) آل عمران, 159. 24) Tafsir Rahnama.

Messages

1- Whoever lacks logic relies on wealth and adornment, considering their possession a sign of truth (Haqq) and their absence a sign of falsehood (Bātil). "فلولا القى علیه"

2- Men adorning themselves with gold is a Pharaonic practice. "القى علیه اسورة من ذهب"

3- Weakening the divine leader and casting doubt (shubhah) is the work of the Pharaoh's people. "او جاء معه الملائكة..."

4- Obedience in a corrupt system is based on the humiliation of the people. "استخف قومه فاطاعوه"

5- Self-abasement and the emptying of identity lead to submission before the tyrants (Tāghūts). "استخفّ قومه فاطاعوه"

6- Blind devotion and obedience are rooted in cultural poverty, narrow-mindedness, and superficiality among humans. "استخفّ قومه فاطاعوه"

7- A society that deviates from the divine path loses faith in itself and becomes susceptible to humiliation. "استخف قومه... انهم كانوا قوما فاسقین" (Fisq (فسق) means deviating from the orbit of truth/Haqq).

8- The one who boasted of the river, "و هذه الانهار تجرى من تحتى" (And these rivers flow beneath me), was drowned in that very river. "اغرقناهم"

9- Sometimes punishments occur in this world in addition to the Hereafter. "اغرقناهم"

10- In the sphere of transgression (fisq) and tyranny (ṭughyān), the commander and the commanded are destroyed together. "اجمعین"

11- Allah's wrath and vengeance upon man are due to man's own actions. "فاطاعوه... آسفونا... انتقمنا منهم"

12- According to historical evidence, the destruction of tyrannical powers is an inevitable divine tradition (Sunnah). "فجعلناهم سلفاً و مثلاً للاخرین"

13- The events of the past serve as a lesson for future generations. "اغرقناهم... جعلناهم سلفاً و مثلاً"

Ayahs

۞ وَلَمَّا ضُرِبَ ٱبْنُ مَرْيَمَ مَثَلًا إِذَا قَوْمُكَ مِنْهُ يَصِدُّونَ (٥۷)
When the Son of Mary was cited as an example, behold, your people raise an outcry. (57)
وَقَالُوٓا۟ ءَأَـٰلِهَتُنَا خَيْرٌ أَمْ هُوَ ۚ مَا ضَرَبُوهُ لَكَ إِلَّا جَدَلًۢا ۚ بَلْ هُمْ قَوْمٌ خَصِمُونَ (٥۸)
They say, ‘Are our gods better or he?’ They cite him to you only for the sake of contention. Indeed, they are a contentious lot. (58)

Key Points

When Ayah 98 of Surah Al-Anbiya was revealed, stating: «انّكم و ما تعبدون من دون اللّه حصب جهنّم» (Indeed, you and what you worship other than Allah are the fuel of Hell), the idolaters, along with the idols and deities, would become the fuel of Hell. A person asked the Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله: If this is the case, then Jesus ('Isa) and Uzayr (Ezra) are also worshipped by the Christians and Jews, and according to this Ayah, Jesus and Uzayr will be the fuel of Hell. If Jesus is in Hell, then we and our idols accept that Hell. The disbelievers cheered and laughed. The Prophet said: Among humans, only those deities will go to Hell who desired to be worshipped, such as Pharaoh. But Jesus and Uzayr never agreed to be worshipped. Furthermore, in the continuation of the same Surah, the account of these distinguished individuals is separated from the account of the idols, as He says: «انّ الّذین سَبقت لهم منّا الحسنى اولئك عنها مبعدون» (Indeed, those for whom the good word has preceded from Us—those will be removed therefrom [from Hell]). These servants of Allah (who did not agree to be worshipped) and who have been promised good by Us are far removed from Hell. In any case, the polytheists used Jesus as an example, laughed, and thought the Prophet had no answer, but the comparison of Jesus to idols was nothing more than a mere argument (jadal).

The Quran's insistence on introducing حضرت عیسى (Jesus) as the son of Mary is to prevent exaggeration (ghuluw) and the belief that He is the son of God. The only name of a woman mentioned in the Quran is the name Maryam (Mary). The remaining women are not mentioned by name, such as the mother of Moses «امّ موسى» (25), the sister of Moses «اخته» (26), the wife of Imran «امرأة عمران» (27), the wife of Pharaoh «امرأة فرعون» (28), the wife of the Aziz of Egypt «امرأة العزیز» (29), the wife of Lot «امرأة لوط» (30), the wife of Abu Lahab «امرأته حمّالة الحطب» (31), the wives of the Prophet «نساء النبى» (32), the wife of Noah «امرأة نوح» (33), and the woman who used to weave. «التى نقضت غزلها» (34)

25) قصص، ۷. 26) قصص، ۱۱. 27) آل عمران، ۳۵. 28) قصص، ۹. 29) یوسف، ۳۰. 30) تحریم، ۱۰. 31) تبّت، ۲. 32) احزاب، ۳۰. 33) تحریم، ۱۰. 34) نحل، ۹۹.

Messages

1- Extremism and negligence in beliefs reach a point where one group elevates Jesus to the level of divinity, and another group places Him on par with stone idols. «ضرب ابن مریم مثلاً»

2- Disbelievers welcome any scene of uproar that can be used as a means to strike at the Prophet. «اذا قومك منه یصدون» (As soon as someone said: If the deities are destined for Hell, then Jesus is also destined for Hell, they laughed with cheers.)

3- Good argumentation (jidal) is valuable, but hostile argumentation is condemned. «بل هم قوم خصمون»

Ayahs

إِنْ هُوَ إِلَّا عَبْدٌ أَنْعَمْنَا عَلَيْهِ وَجَعَلْنَـٰهُ مَثَلًا لِّبَنِىٓ إِسْرَٰٓءِيلَ (٥۹)
He was just a servant whom We had blessed and made an exemplar for the Children of Israel. (59)
وَلَوْ نَشَآءُ لَجَعَلْنَا مِنكُم مَّلَـٰٓئِكَةً فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ يَخْلُفُونَ (٦٠)
Had We wished We would have set angels in your stead to be [your] successors on the earth. (60)

Key Points

Defense of the infallible personalities is necessary, and those great figures should not be used as a pretext for malicious goals. The polytheists cited Jesus (عليه السلام) as an example, saying He is the object of worship for a group, and according to the Quran, the worshiper and the non-divine object of worship are destined for Hell, therefore Jesus must be destined for Hell. However, the Quran honors Jesus in this Ayah.

Messages

  1. Jesus is not an object of worship, but rather a sincere servant of Allah. «ان هو الا عبد»
  2. Servitude (to Allah) is the cause for receiving divine favors. «عبد انعمنا علیه»
  3. Although Jesus was a Prophet of resolve (Ulul-'Azm) and was sent for all people of His time, His primary audience was the Children of Israel. «مثلاً لبنى اسرائیل»
  4. Allah has no need for the worship of people. If He willed, He would place angels instead of people who would be constantly obedient. «لو نشاء»

Ayahs

وَإِنَّهُۥ لَعِلْمٌ لِّلسَّاعَةِ فَلَا تَمْتَرُنَّ بِهَا وَٱتَّبِعُونِ ۚ هَـٰذَا صِرَٰطٌ مُّسْتَقِيمٌ (٦۱)
[Say,] ‘Indeed he is a portent of the Hour; so do not doubt it and follow me. This is a straight path. (61)
وَلَا يَصُدَّنَّكُمُ ٱلشَّيْطَـٰنُ ۖ إِنَّهُۥ لَكُمْ عَدُوٌّ مُّبِينٌ (٦۲)
Do not let Satan bar you [from the way of Allah]. Indeed he is your manifest enemy.’ (62)

Key Points

In response to the polytheists who considered Jesus an example of non-divine deities, this Ayah states: Jesus Himself is one of the signs of the Hour (of Resurrection). His birth from a mother without a spouse is a sign of Allah's power to recreate humans on the Day of Judgment, just as He used to revive the dead in this world. Furthermore, at the end of the world's life, He will descend from the heavens, which is a prelude to the Resurrection.

Messages

1- The existence of the friends of Allah is a sign of the Hour and a reminder of it. «لعلم للساعة»

2- Upon seeing the signs of Divine power, doubt regarding the possibility of the dead being resurrected is unwarranted. «فلا تمترنّ»

3- The infallible leaders are upon the straight path, and following them is also the straight path. «اتبعون هذا صراط مستقیم»

4- Man instinctively seeks the path, which Allah shows him. «هذا صراط مستقیم»

5- Undue doubt causes one to stray from the straight path. «فلا تمترنّ... واتبعون هذا صراط مستقیم»

6- Man needs a role model to move along the straight path. «واتبعون هذا صراط مستقیم»

7- The straight path is only one path. «هذا صراط مستقیم»

8- Satan lies in wait for those who traverse the straight path. «هذا صراط مستقیم - ولا یصدنكم الشیطان»

9- Given Satan's history of deceiving Adam and Eve, his enmity is hidden from no one. «عدوّ مبین»

10- Recognizing Satan's temptations does not require deep contemplation or intellect, because human nature easily understands deviation. «عدوّ مبین»

Ayah

وَلَمَّا جَآءَ عِيسَىٰ بِٱلْبَيِّنَـٰتِ قَالَ قَدْ جِئْتُكُم بِٱلْحِكْمَةِ وَلِأُبَيِّنَ لَكُم بَعْضَ ٱلَّذِى تَخْتَلِفُونَ فِيهِ ۖ فَٱتَّقُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ وَأَطِيعُونِ (٦۳)
When Jesus brought those manifest proofs, he said, ‘I have certainly brought you wisdom, and [I have come] to make clear to you some of the things that you differ about. So be wary of Allah and obey me. (63)

Key Points

Among the reasons for the sending of the Prophets, the following can be named: 1. Giving insight to the people. ("حكمت") 2. Clarification regarding matters of dispute. ("لاُبیّن") 3. Invitation to piety (Taqwa). ("واتّقوا اللّه") 4. Introducing the true model/example. ("و اطیعون")

Weakness and instability are not found in the work of Allah, because He is Wise (Hakim), His Book is Wise, and the logic of His Prophets is wisdom (Hikmah).

Divine piety (Taqwa) encompasses faith in Allah, performing obligations (Wajibat), and abandoning prohibitions (Muharramat). Following the Prophet involves accepting the principle of prophethood (Nubuwwah) and the tradition (Sunnah) of that great personality.

Messages

1- The Prophets came accompanied by clear, convincing, rational, undeniable proofs, and numerous miracles. ("جاء عیسى بالبیّنات... بالحكمة...")

2- The movement of the Prophets is a cultural and intellectual movement, not a coup d'état. ("جئتكم بالحكمة")

3- Resolving religious disputes is among the goals of the Prophets. ("لابیّن لكم... تختلفون فیه")

4- Piety (Taqwa) and obedience to divine leaders guarantee social unity. ("لابیّن لكم بعض الّذى تختلفون فیه فاتقوا اللّه و اطیعون")

Ayahs

إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ هُوَ رَبِّى وَرَبُّكُمْ فَٱعْبُدُوهُ ۚ هَـٰذَا صِرَٰطٌ مُّسْتَقِيمٌ (٦٤)
Indeed Allah is my Lord and your Lord; so worship Him. This is a straight path.’ (64)
فَٱخْتَلَفَ ٱلْأَحْزَابُ مِنۢ بَيْنِهِمْ ۖ فَوَيْلٌ لِّلَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوا۟ مِنْ عَذَابِ يَوْمٍ أَلِيمٍ (٦٥)
But the factions differed among themselves. So woe to the wrongdoers for the punishment of a painful day. (65)

Key Points

Prophet Jesus عليه السلام had the duty to resolve some of the differences, but as soon as He started His mission, new differences arose concerning Him Himself. «لابیّن لكم بعض الّذى تختلفون فیه... فاختلف الاحزاب من بینهم»

Messages

1- The most fundamental message of Prophet Jesus was inviting people to monotheism (Tawhid). «ان اللّه هو ربّى و ربكم»

2- Supervision and management of affairs belong solely to Allah. «ان اللّه هو ربّى»

3- Worship is exclusive to the One in whose hand all affairs rest. «ربّى و ربّكم فاعبدوه»

4- Worshipping anything or anyone other than Allah is straying from the path. «فاعبدوه هذا صراط مستقیم»

5- Servitude to Allah and following the heavenly leader are one reality. «و اطیعون هذا صراط مستقیم»(35), «فاعبده... هذا صراط مستقیم»

6- Disagreement causes deviation from the straight path. «هذا صراط مستقیم - فاختلف الاحزاب»

7- Creating division and disagreement after the clarification provided by the Prophets is oppression (zulm). «فاختلف الاحزاب... فویل للّذین ظلموا» 35) Az-Zukhruf, 61.

Ayahs

هَلْ يَنظُرُونَ إِلَّا ٱلسَّاعَةَ أَن تَأْتِيَهُم بَغْتَةً وَهُمْ لَا يَشْعُرُونَ (٦٦)
Do they not consider that the Hour may overtake them suddenly while they are unaware? (66)
ٱلْأَخِلَّآءُ يَوْمَئِذٍۭ بَعْضُهُمْ لِبَعْضٍ عَدُوٌّ إِلَّا ٱلْمُتَّقِينَ (٦۷)
On that day, friends will be one another’s enemies, except for the Godwary. (67)

Key Points

The state of those who chose friends based on criteria other than piety (Taqwa) will be thus in the Hereafter: - Regret and sorrow over why they befriended him. «یا ویلتى لیتنى لم اتّخذ فلاناً خلیلا»(36) Woe unto me! Would that I had not taken such-and-such a one as a friend. - Friends will have no role in helping one another. «و لا یسئل حمیم حمیماً»(37) - Friends will become enemies to one another. «الاخلاء یومئذ بعضهم لبعض عدوّ» (the Ayah under discussion) - They will curse one another. «كلّما دخلت امّة لعنت اختها»(38) - They will flee from one another. «یفرّ المرء من اخیه و اُمّه و ابیه و صاحبته و بنیه»(39) - They will blame their sins on one another. «لولا انتم لكنّا مؤمنین»(40) - They will disavow one another. «یا لیت بینى و بینك بعد المشرقین»(41)

36) Al-Furqan, 28. 37) Al-Ma'arij, 10. 38) Al-A'raf, 38. 39) Abasa, 34 - 36. 40) Saba, 31. 41) Az-Zukhruf, 38.

Messages

1- The occurrence of the Hour (Qiyamah) is sudden, and its time is unknown to anyone. «بغتة وهم لایشعرون»

2- All friendships that are not based on piety (Taqwa) will turn into enmity. «الاخلاء... عدوّ الا المتّقین»

Ayahs

يَـٰعِبَادِ لَا خَوْفٌ عَلَيْكُمُ ٱلْيَوْمَ وَلَآ أَنتُمْ تَحْزَنُونَ (٦۸)
[They will be told,] ‘O My servants! Today you will have no fear, nor will you grieve (68)
ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ بِـَٔايَـٰتِنَا وَكَانُوا۟ مُسْلِمِينَ (٦۹)
—those who believed in Our signs and had been Muslims. (69)
ٱدْخُلُوا۟ ٱلْجَنَّةَ أَنتُمْ وَأَزْوَٰجُكُمْ تُحْبَرُونَ (۷٠)
Enter paradise, you and your spouses, rejoicing.’ (70)
يُطَافُ عَلَيْهِم بِصِحَافٍ مِّن ذَهَبٍ وَأَكْوَابٍ ۖ وَفِيهَا مَا تَشْتَهِيهِ ٱلْأَنفُسُ وَتَلَذُّ ٱلْأَعْيُنُ ۖ وَأَنتُمْ فِيهَا خَـٰلِدُونَ (۷۱)
They will be served around with golden dishes and goblets, and therein will be whatever the souls desire and eyes delight in. ‘You will remain in it [forever]. (71)

Key Points

«تحبرون» از «حبره» به معناى حال پسندیده است كه به صورت شادمانى بر چهره نمودار مى شود، «صحاف» جمع «صحفه» به ظرف هاى بزرگ گفته مى شود. «اكواب» جمع «كوب» به جامى گفته مى شود كه دسته ندارد. Tuhbarūn (تحبرون) is derived from habrah (حبره), meaning a desirable state that manifests as joy on the face. Sihāf (صحاف), the plural of sahfah (صحفه), refers to large vessels. Akwāb (اكواب), the plural of kūb (كوب), refers to a goblet that has no handle. Yeki az لذّت هاى چشم، ملاقات اولیاى الهى است كه انسان در دنیا موفّق به دیدار آنان نشده است. One of the pleasures for the eye is meeting the Divine saints (Awliyā' Allāh) whom a person was not successful in meeting in this world. The blessings of Paradise possess various advantages: 1. They satisfy all tastes and eyes. «تشتهیه الانفس و تلذّ الاعین» 2. They are diverse. «صحاف - اكواب» 3. They are beautiful. «ذهب» 4. They are according to desire. «تشتهیه الانفس» 5. The eye does not tire of seeing them. «تلذّ الاعین» Since the Quran commands, "Enter Paradise, you and your spouses," it becomes clear that the intended meaning is their worldly spouses, because the Hūr al-'Īn (maidens of Paradise) are not outside Paradise such that they would be commanded to enter it.(42)

42) Tafsīr al-Mīzān.

Messages

1- Servitude to Allah is the secret to being safeguarded from fears and dangers on the Day of Resurrection. «یا عباد لاخوف علیكم...»

2- First tranquility, then the reception of the blessings of Paradise. «لا خوف علیهم... ادخلوا الجنّة»

3- Faith alone is not sufficient; we must have complete submission. «آمنوا... و كانوا مسلمین»

4- Worldly believing spouses will also be companions in the Hereafter. «ادخلوا... انتم و ازواجكم»

5- The pleasure of the eye on the Day of Resurrection is such that it equals the other desires of the people of Paradise. «تشتهیه الانفس و تلذّ الاعین»

6- Attaining all desires is only possible in Paradise. «فیها ما تشتهیه الانفس»

7- Human instincts and inner attractions accompany man until the Day of Resurrection. «فیها ما تشتهیه الانفس...»

Ayahs

وَتِلْكَ ٱلْجَنَّةُ ٱلَّتِىٓ أُورِثْتُمُوهَا بِمَا كُنتُمْ تَعْمَلُونَ (۷۲)
That is the paradise you have been given to inherit for what you used to do. (72)
لَكُمْ فِيهَا فَـٰكِهَةٌ كَثِيرَةٌ مِّنْهَا تَأْكُلُونَ (۷۳)
There are abundant fruits for you in it, from which you will eat.’ (73)

Key Points

According to narrations, Allah has designated a place in Hell and a station in Paradise for every human being. The people of Paradise inherit the paradisiacal station of the people of Hell, and the people of Hell inherit the hellish station of the people of Paradise.(43) The issue of inheriting Paradise has been mentioned in various verses: «اولئك هم الوارثون الّذین یرثون الفردوس»(44)

43) Tafsir Maraghi and Majma‘ al-Bayan. 44) Al-Mu’minun, 10 and 11.

Messages

1- Paradise is enduring in exchange for good deeds. «بما كنتم تعملون»

2- The multiplicity and variety of fruits is one of the blessings of Paradise. «فاكهة كثیرة»

3- The pleasures of Paradise, like the pleasures of this world, are in harmony with human instincts. «منها تأكلون»

Ayahs

إِنَّ ٱلْمُجْرِمِينَ فِى عَذَابِ جَهَنَّمَ خَـٰلِدُونَ (۷٤)
Indeed the guilty will remain [forever] in the punishment of hell. (74)
لَا يُفَتَّرُ عَنْهُمْ وَهُمْ فِيهِ مُبْلِسُونَ (۷٥)
It will not be lightened for them, and they will be despondent in it. (75)
وَمَا ظَلَمْنَـٰهُمْ وَلَـٰكِن كَانُوا۟ هُمُ ٱلظَّـٰلِمِينَ (۷٦)
We did not wrong them, but they themselves were wrongdoers. (76)

Key Points

Mublisūn (مبلسون) is derived from iblās (ابلاس), meaning sorrow accompanied by despair. Perhaps the word Iblīs (ابلیس) is also applied to Satan because of his despair (yās) of Allah's mercy. Eternity, non-diminution (of punishment), and despair and sorrow are among the signs of Hellfire (Dūzakh).

Messages

1- In education (tarbiyah), warning and glad tidings must be side by side. «تلك الجنّة - المجرمین فى عذاب»

2- The factor for entering Hellfire is the performance (actions) of the person himself. «ان المجرمین»

3- The punishments of Allah are just. «و ما ظلمناهم»

4- Continuous oppression results in continuous punishment. «خالدون... كانوا هم الظالمین»

Ayah

وَنَادَوْا۟ يَـٰمَـٰلِكُ لِيَقْضِ عَلَيْنَا رَبُّكَ ۖ قَالَ إِنَّكُم مَّـٰكِثُونَ (۷۷)
They will call out, ‘O Malik! Let your Lord finish us off!’ He will say, ‘Indeed you will stay on.’ (77)

Key Points

On the Day of Resurrection, the inhabitants of Hell seek help from everyone: - Sometimes from the believers. «انظرونا نقتبس من نوركم» (O inhabitants of Paradise!) Look upon us so that we may acquire light from you. - Sometimes from the oppressive leaders. «فهل انتم مغنون» Will you deliver us from the wrath of Allah? - Sometimes from the guardians of Hell. (Similar to this very Ayah) The inhabitants of Hell wish for annihilation, but it is not granted. As we read elsewhere: «لایقضى علیهم فیموتوا»(45) Death is not decreed upon them so that they may die.

45) Fatir, 36.

Messages

1- Man's situation reaches a point where he seeks help from the guardian of Hell. «یا مالك»

2- The wish of the inhabitants of Hell is death and annihilation. «لیقض علینا»

3- The inhabitants of Hell are permanent residents in the torment. «انكم ماكثون»

Ayahs

لَقَدْ جِئْنَـٰكُم بِٱلْحَقِّ وَلَـٰكِنَّ أَكْثَرَكُمْ لِلْحَقِّ كَـٰرِهُونَ (۷۸)
‘We certainly brought you the truth, but most of you were averse to the truth.’ (78)
أَمْ أَبْرَمُوٓا۟ أَمْرًا فَإِنَّا مُبْرِمُونَ (۷۹)
Have they settled on some [devious] plan? Indeed, We too are settling [on Our plans]. (79)
أَمْ يَحْسَبُونَ أَنَّا لَا نَسْمَعُ سِرَّهُمْ وَنَجْوَىٰهُم ۚ بَلَىٰ وَرُسُلُنَا لَدَيْهِمْ يَكْتُبُونَ (۸٠)
Do they suppose that We do not hear their secret thoughts and their secret talks? Yes indeed [We do]! And with them are Our messengers, writing down [everything]. (80)

Key Points

The word "Ibrām" (إبرام) means to confirm or strengthen, and here it signifies a serious determination to plot against the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله and reject his religion.

Messages

1- Divine punishment follows the completion of the argument (proof). «انكم ماكثون، لقد جئناكم بالحقّ»

2- Those who accept the truth are few. «جئناكم بالحقّ... اكثركم للحق كارهون»

3- The source of truth is Allah, and the criterion for reward and punishment is the acceptance or rejection of the truth. «جئناكم بالحقّ... للحقّ كارهون»

4- Allah does not punish until a decision or action originates from us; Divine punishment is commensurate with our performance. «ابرموا... مبرمون»

5- Deniers of the Prophets should know that they are dealing with Allah. «ابرموا امراً فانّا مبرمون»

6- The disbelievers act only based on conjecture and speculation. «ام یحسبون»

7- The opponents have not recognized Allah, as they imagine that He is unaware of their secrets. «ام یحسبون انا لا نسمع سرّهم»

8- Every word is recorded and written down by the angels. «رسلنا... یكتبون»

Ayahs

قُلْ إِن كَانَ لِلرَّحْمَـٰنِ وَلَدٌ فَأَنَا۠ أَوَّلُ ٱلْعَـٰبِدِينَ (۸۱)
Say, ‘If the All-beneficent had offspring, I would have been the first to worship [him].’ (81)
سُبْحَـٰنَ رَبِّ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ رَبِّ ٱلْعَرْشِ عَمَّا يَصِفُونَ (۸۲)
Clear is the Lord of the heavens and the earth, the Lord of the Throne, of whatever they allege [concerning Him]! (82)

Messages

1- We should show tolerance and leniency when speaking with opponents. «ان كان للرحمن ولد» This phrase is used for the sake of concession in the discussion; otherwise, Allah has no son, and the phrase «لو كانوا» should have been used.

2- Whenever there is a possibility of imperfection being attributed to Allah in a statement, it is appropriate that we declare Him transcendent. «ان كان للرحمن ولد... سبحان»

3- What need does the Lord of existence have for a son? «ربّ السموات والارض رب العرش»

Ayahs

فَذَرْهُمْ يَخُوضُوا۟ وَيَلْعَبُوا۟ حَتَّىٰ يُلَـٰقُوا۟ يَوْمَهُمُ ٱلَّذِى يُوعَدُونَ (۸۳)
So leave them to gossip and play until they encounter the day they are promised. (83)
وَهُوَ ٱلَّذِى فِى ٱلسَّمَآءِ إِلَـٰهٌ وَفِى ٱلْأَرْضِ إِلَـٰهٌ ۚ وَهُوَ ٱلْحَكِيمُ ٱلْعَلِيمُ (۸٤)
It is He who is God in the sky, and God on the earth, and He is the All-Wise, the All-Knowing. (84)

Key Points

Where reasoning and warning are ineffective, the opponent must be left to their own devices, just as a compassionate physician, upon despair, leaves the patient to his own state.

Messages

1- One of the Divine traditions is granting respite to the unworthy and leaving them to their own state after the completion of the proof. «فذرهم...»

2- Unjust beliefs, thoughts, and deeds are a quagmire that drowns individuals. «یخوضوا»

3- Not every investigation and research is valuable. Sometimes a person, even while researching, is merely playing. «یخضوا و یلعبوا»

4- Whatever is not within the orbit of truth and logic is a type of play. «یخوضوا و یلعبوا»

5- Those who speak nonsense have a bitter day ahead of them. «یومهم الّذى یوعدون»

6- The disbelief of people has no effect on the greatness and glory of Allah. «فذرهم... و هو الّذى فى السماء اله و فى الارض اله»

7- Monotheism was the main axis of the disagreement between the Prophet and the polytheists. (The repetition of the word «اله» indicates this). «فى السماء اله و فى الارض اله»

8- Absolute wisdom and knowledge belong exclusively to Allah. «وهو الحكیم العلیم»

9- The One worthy of worship is He Who possesses infinite knowledge and wisdom. «اله... هو الحكیم العلیم»

10- Wisdom is different from knowledge. How many scholars are there who are not people of wisdom; they possess knowledge but lack insight. Allah is both All-Knowing and All-Wise. «وهو الحكیم العلیم»

Ayah

وَتَبَارَكَ ٱلَّذِى لَهُۥ مُلْكُ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ وَمَا بَيْنَهُمَا وَعِندَهُۥ عِلْمُ ٱلسَّاعَةِ وَإِلَيْهِ تُرْجَعُونَ (۸٥)
Blessed is He to whom belongs the kingdom of the heavens and the earth and whatever is between them, and with Him is the knowledge of the Hour, and to Him you will be brought back. (85)

Key Points

The term "تبارك" (Tabārak) is derived from "بركت" (Barakah), meaning exaltation (ta'ālā). That is, Allah is highly exalted. In the Quran, Allah is described nine times using the phrase "تبارك". In this Ayah and the previous Ayah, eight perfections are presented for Allah so that the belief in Him having a son and other things unjustly attributed to Him are refuted.

Messages

1- The sovereignty of the One Allah over the system of existence is enduring and imperishable. «تبارك الّذى له ملك السموات»

2- Allah is free from every deficiency and blessed in relation to every perfection. «سبحان... تبارك...»

3- In the face of others' misconceptions, one must glorify the sacred things. «تبارك الّذى له...»

4- Allah is both the Origin and the Destination. «له ملك السموات والارض... الیه ترجعون»

5- No one except Allah is aware of the time the Hour will occur. «و عنده علم الساعة»

6- The summoning of people on the Day of Judgment is both universal and compulsory. «الیه ترجعون»

Ayah

وَلَا يَمْلِكُ ٱلَّذِينَ يَدْعُونَ مِن دُونِهِ ٱلشَّفَـٰعَةَ إِلَّا مَن شَهِدَ بِٱلْحَقِّ وَهُمْ يَعْلَمُونَ (۸٦)
Those whom they invoke besides Him have no power of intercession, except those who are witness to the truth and who know [for whom to intercede]. (86)

Messages

1- The principle of intercession (Shafa'ah) is accepted, but false intercessions are rejected. «لا یملك... الشفاعة»

2- Reform must begin at the source. The source of polytheism (Shirk) is the hope for intercession from false deities, and people must be made despairing of such a notion. «لا یملك الّذین یدعون من دونه»

3- The one who has the right to intercede is the one who acknowledges and bears witness to the truth (Haqq). «شهد بالحق»

4- Those who have the right to intercede do not intercede for everyone or in every case; they themselves know where intercession is appropriate. «و هم یعلمون»

5- Testimony and witnessing are valuable only when they are based on knowledge. «شهد بالحقّ و هم یعلمون»

Ayah

وَلَئِن سَأَلْتَهُم مَّنْ خَلَقَهُمْ لَيَقُولُنَّ ٱللَّهُ ۖ فَأَنَّىٰ يُؤْفَكُونَ (۸۷)
If you ask them, ‘Who created them?’ they will surely say, ‘Allah.’ Then where do they stray? (87)

Key Points

The word "یوفكون" (yu'fakūn) derives from "اِفك" (ifk), meaning turning away and deviation from the natural path.

Messages

1- The polytheists of Mecca accepted Allah's Creatorship, but they were afflicted by polytheism regarding Lordship, governance, problem-solving, and intercession, considering others to be involved. «لئن سئلتهم من خلقهم لیقولن اللّه»

2- Monotheism is an innate matter, and polytheism is a deviation from human nature. «فانّى یؤفكون»

Ayah

وَقِيلِهِۦ يَـٰرَبِّ إِنَّ هَـٰٓؤُلَآءِ قَوْمٌ لَّا يُؤْمِنُونَ (۸۸)
And his plaint: ‘My Lord! Indeed these are a people who will not have faith!’ (88)

Key Points

«قیله» is a verbal noun (masdar) like «قوله», meaning "speech" or "saying."

Messages

1- Prophets seek refuge in Allah when facing obstinate individuals. «یا ربّ ان هؤلاء قوم لا یؤمنون»

2- Religious preachers should not expect acceptance of the truth from all people. «هؤلاء قوم لا یؤمنون»

Ayah

فَٱصْفَحْ عَنْهُمْ وَقُلْ سَلَـٰمٌ ۚ فَسَوْفَ يَعْلَمُونَ (۸۹)
So disregard them, and say, ‘Peace!’ Soon they will know. (89)

Key Points

In Arab culture, greetings (salām) are given on two occasions: one upon entering and meeting, and the other upon leaving and saying farewell. Just as we say at the end of every prayer: «السلام علیكم و رحمة اللّه و بركاته». The intent of salām in this Ayah is the salām of farewell and separation (cessation of interaction). In Persian, too, we have dorūd (greeting) and badrūd (farewell).

Messages

1- Leave the stubborn polytheists to their own state and do not dispute with them. «فاصفح عنهم»

2- Even with polytheists, a life of peaceful coexistence is necessary. «فاصفح... و قل سلام» (The minimum expectation of religion from humanity is peaceful social coexistence.)

3- The Prophet of Islam carried out all his actions by the command of Allah. «فاصفح عنهم»

4- Allah has warned the opponents of the prophets of a severe punishment. «فسوف یعلمون»
